name: Hewlett-Packard
status_note: Split into HP Inc. (HPQ) and Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) on November 1, 2015

name: HP Inc.
status_note: Consumer PCs and printers successor to Hewlett-Packard post-2015 split

name: Hewlett Packard Enterprise
status_note: Enterprise hardware/software/services successor to Hewlett-Packard post-2015 split

name: Aberdeen Group
status_note: IT market research firm; acquired by Aberdeen (Harte-Hanks spinoff) lineage; now part of Aberdeen Strategy & Research

name: Taligent
status_note: IBM/Apple/HP joint venture dissolved January 1998; engineering teams absorbed into IBM

name: Micro Focus
status_note: COBOL tooling partner referenced in study; acquired by OpenText January 31, 2023

name: OpenText
status_note: Acquired Micro Focus in January 2023; current owner of Micro Focus product portfolio

name: Open Software Foundation (OSF)
status_note: Merged with X/Open in 1996 to form The Open Group; previously merged with Unix International in 1994

name: The Open Group
status_note: Formed 1996 from merger of X/Open and OSF; manages UNIX trademark and DCE standards

name: Object Management Group (OMG)
status_note: Maintains CORBA and other OO standards; still active as of 2026

name: Sun Microsystems
status_note: Referenced as Solaris host platform; acquired by Oracle Corporation January 27, 2010

name: Oracle Corporation
status_note: Acquired Sun Microsystems in 2010; current owner of Solaris and related assets

name: IBM
status_note: Partnered with HP/Apple in Taligent; absorbed Taligent in 1998; still active as of 2026

name: Informix
status_note: Database vendor referenced as supported DB; acquired by IBM in 2001; IBM ended continuing support for Informix April 30, 2025

name: Apple Computer
status_note: Co-founded Taligent with IBM; now Apple Inc.; still active as of 2026
